'''Vonya''' was a [[Witches of Dathomir|Witch]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] and princess of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsister]] clan in [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]]. She was second in command of the clan led by the Nightsister [[Mother (title)/Legends|Mother]], [[Zalem/Legends|Zalem]].

==Biography==
Vonya was involved in the plot to reopen the [[Infinity Gate]] that lay under the [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomiri]] surface. This plot was complicated by the arrival of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], who was [[Mission to Dathomir (Post–Naboo crisis)|investigating]] the disappearance of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ova]]. During this time, she and her fellow witches ridiculed Zalem's misshapen daughter, [[Ros Lai|"the Rancor"]]. During a raid led by another Nightsister princess, [[Yongti]], Vonya was able to kill her with a [[Subterine]] and then executed her followers.<ref name="Republic 23">[[Star Wars (1998) 23|''Star Wars'' (1998) 23]]</ref>

As she and Zalem approached the Star chamber, they came under attack by a giant [[whuffa worm]] that guarding the chamber. She brought Vos before Zalem, and punished Ros Lai for not guarding slaves against Yongti.<ref name="Republic 24">[[Star Wars (1998) 24|''Star Wars'' (1998) 24]]</ref> Vonya then discovered Ros Lai's true form and tried to match her power with her own, but was stopped by Vos. She then vanished and informed Zalem of this. They than captured Vos and tortured him with [[artery worm]]s until Ros Lai freed him and escaped.<ref name="Republic 25">[[Star Wars (1998) 25|''Star Wars'' (1998) 25]]</ref>

Vonya then pursued Vos, Ros Lai, and [[Yag Shushin]] to the star chamber, and appeared to be destroyed by them. Vonya was then [[Death/Legends|killed]] when Quin destroyed her ship after she killed his friend Yag.<ref name="Republic 26">[[Star Wars (1998) 26|''Star Wars'' (1998) 26]]</ref>
